plan_section: Step 1: Brand foundation, runtime configuration, and URL baseline

plan_alignment.notes: The staged changes establish shared Colorado MeshCore brand/runtime constants, update app metadata, JSON-LD, RSS, manifest, robots, headers comments, Netlify runtime comments, and GitHub Actions Node versions as required. Sitemap was not staged but already consumes BASE_URL, so it will emit the new Colorado URL from the constants change.

correctness_safety.notes: metadataBase remains absolute by default, JSON-LD URLs are formed from the normalized base URL and existing logo path, no secrets are exposed, and existing named constants/API route exports remain available for current callers.

code_quality.notes: The constants module is cleaner and maintains backwards-compatible exports. Metadata and schema generation now consume shared constants instead of duplicated strings.

completeness.notes: Step acceptance criteria are satisfied for brand constants, metadata, manifest, feed, robots, CI/security Node 24 baseline, typecheck script, and package engine metadata. Implementer reported lint, typecheck, build, grep guard, and diff whitespace verification passing.

patterns.notes: Changes follow the planned central brand/runtime source-of-truth pattern without migrating out-of-scope visual shell/navigation/footer content reserved for later steps.

integration.notes: Imports/exports are consistent, package-lock root engine metadata mirrors package.json, CI uses the new typecheck script, and existing consumers of BASE_URL and other constants continue to compile.

flags.note: This unmodified Netlify helper file still has a Denver MeshCore comment, but it is outside the staged Step 1 file list and not user-visible metadata/navigation; later Netlify/Docker cleanup can decide whether to update or retire it.
